claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 3 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin. To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

**First, let's start with your child's age:**

How old is the child you're claiming for? Please tell me if they are:
- Under 16 years old
- Between 16 and 19 years old
- 20 years old or over

If you have multiple children, we can go through each one. Let's start with one child first.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I've got three children - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 2, and Charlie who's also 3. So they're all under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have three young children - Alex (3), Blake (2), and Charlie (3). Since they're all under 16, that's a good start.

Let me continue with the assessment. For children under 16, I need to check a few more things.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now, the next question:

**Are all three children working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Given that Alex, Blake, and Charlie are all aged 2-3 years old, I assume they're not working, but I need to confirm this for the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them work at all - they're just toddlers!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are any of your children receiving benefits in their own right** (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

Again, given their young ages, I assume this doesn't apply, but I need to confirm this for the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them receive any benefits in their own name. They're too young for that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely right. Let me move forward.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are any of your children in an apprenticeship?**

Given that they're toddlers (ages 2-3), I assume they're not in any apprenticeships, but I need to confirm for completeness.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not - they're only 2 and 3 years old!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now let's move on to questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You would qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**, OR
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, OR
3. You have pre-settled status and are working/actively seeking work/have sufficient resources/studying, OR
4. You're living abroad as a Crown servant

The most common situation is simply normally living in the UK. Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Living in Manchester means you meet the residency requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now, an important question about your responsibility for the children:

**Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

You would qualify if ANY of the following apply:

1. **You live with the children**, OR
2. You contribute to their upkeep (paying at least the amount of Child Benefit towards their care - things like food, clothes, pocket money, housing), OR
3. You pay maintenance under a court order or agreement

Do any of these apply to you for all three children?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Blake lives with me at home. But Alex and Charlie don't live with me - they live with their other parent. I do help out with Alex though - I contribute money towards their upkeep for things like clothes and expenses.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is important information. Let me make sure I understand correctly:

- **Blake (age 2)** - lives with you at home ✓
- **Alex (age 3)** - doesn't live with you, but you contribute money towards their upkeep ✓
- **Charlie (age 3)** - doesn't live with you

For Blake and Alex, it sounds like you meet the responsibility requirement (Blake because you live together, Alex because you contribute to their upkeep).

**For Charlie:** You mentioned Charlie doesn't live with you. Do you contribute any money towards Charlie's upkeep (such as payments for food, clothes, expenses, etc.) or pay any maintenance for Charlie?

This is important because to claim Child Benefit for a child, you need to either live with them OR contribute to their care.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't contribute anything for Charlie. Their mum claims it and they live with her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. That's very helpful to understand your situation.

So to summarize:
- **Blake** - lives with you ✓ (you're responsible)
- **Alex** - you contribute to their upkeep ✓ (you're responsible)
- **Charlie** - lives with their mum, you don't contribute (their mum is responsible and claims Child Benefit)

Let me continue the assessment for Blake and Alex. Since you meet the responsibility requirement for these two children, let me check the next criteria.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about other claimants:

**For Blake and Alex:**

1. **Blake** (who lives with you) - Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake?

2. **Alex** (who you contribute to) - You mentioned Alex lives with their other parent. Is that parent currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is anyone else claiming for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
No one else is claiming for Blake - he lives with me and I'm the only one claiming. 

For Alex, no one else is claiming for them either. I contribute towards their upkeep and I'd like to claim for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So for both Blake and Alex, there are no other claimants - that's good.

Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about the children's living situations:

**For Blake and Alex, do any of these special circumstances apply?**

- Are either of them currently in hospital?
- Are either of them in local authority care?
- Are either of them in foster care?
- Or are they living in normal circumstances (at home with family)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Blake lives at home with me, and Alex lives with their other parent. Both are just living in normal family situations.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes things straightforward. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
One final question:

**Is the local council paying towards Blake's or Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the council providing financial support for their living costs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything for either of them. They're just living with family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me move to the final step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Based on my assessment, I can now provide you with the outcome for each child:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## **Your Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment Results**

### ✅ **ELIGIBLE for Blake**
You can claim Child Benefit for Blake because you meet all the requirements:
- Blake is under 16 years old
- You live with Blake in Manchester
- No one else is claiming for Blake
- All other eligibility criteria are met

### ✅ **ELIGIBLE for Alex**  
You can claim Child Benefit for Alex because you meet all the requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for Alex through contributing money towards their upkeep (clothes and expenses)
- No one else is claiming for Alex
- All other eligibility criteria are met

### ❌ **NOT ELIGIBLE for Charlie**
Unfortunately, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Charlie because:
- Charlie doesn't live with you
- You don't contribute to Charlie's upkeep
- To claim Child Benefit, you must either live with the child OR contribute to their care
- Charlie's mother is the appropriate claimant

---

## **Next Steps for Blake and Alex:**

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and each child
3. **Provide birth certificates** for Blake and Alex
4. **Be aware:** If you or your partner earn over £60,000, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply
5. **Payments:** Child Benefit is paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

You can apply here: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit claim?

reasonings: Blake is eligible because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old, (2) Blake is not in paid work, (3) Blake is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Blake is not in an apprenticeship, (5) You meet UK residency requirements (you live in Manchester), (6) You are responsible for Blake as you live together, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, (8) Blake is living in normal family circumstances with you, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance. Blake meets all eligibility criteria.

Alex is eligible because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old, (2) Alex is not in paid work, (3) Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Alex is not in an apprenticeship, (5) You meet UK residency requirements (you live in Manchester), (6) You are responsible for Alex through contributing money towards their upkeep for clothes and expenses, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (8) Alex is living in normal family circumstances with their other parent, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. Alex meets all eligibility criteria.

Charlie is not eligible for you to claim because you do not meet the responsibility requirement. While Charlie is under 16 and meets the child-related criteria, you stated that Charlie does not live with you and you do not contribute anything towards Charlie's upkeep. To claim Child Benefit for a child, you must either live with them OR contribute to their care. Charlie's mother claims Child Benefit for Charlie and is the responsible person. Therefore, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You can claim Child Benefit for two of your three children. You are eligible to claim for Blake (who lives with you) and Alex (for whom you contribute to their upkeep), as you meet all the necessary requirements for both children. However, you cannot claim for Charlie because you neither live with Charlie nor contribute to their care - Charlie's mother is the responsible claimant.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Blake is eligible because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old, (2) Blake is not in paid work, (3) Blake is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Blake is not in an apprenticeship, (5) You meet UK residency requirements (you live in Manchester), (6) You are responsible for Blake as you live together, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, (8) Blake is living in normal family circumstances with you, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance. Blake meets all eligibility criteria.

Alex is eligible because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old, (2) Alex is not in paid work, (3) Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Alex is not in an apprenticeship, (5) You meet UK residency requirements (you live in Manchester), (6) You are responsible for Alex through contributing money towards their upkeep for clothes and expenses, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (8) Alex is living in normal family circumstances with their other parent, and (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. Alex meets all eligibility criteria.

Charlie is not eligible for you to claim because you do not meet the responsibility requirement. While Charlie is under 16 and meets the child-related criteria, you stated that Charlie does not live with you and you do not contribute anything towards Charlie's upkeep. To claim Child Benefit for a child, you must either live with them OR contribute to their care. Charlie's mother claims Child Benefit for Charlie and is the responsible person. Therefore, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: You can claim Child Benefit for two of your three children. You are eligible to claim for Blake (who lives with you) and Alex (for whom you contribute to their upkeep), as you meet all the necessary requirements for both children. However, you cannot claim for Charlie because you neither live with Charlie nor contribute to their care - Charlie's mother is the responsible claimant.
